implementation.event_api: POST /v1/policy-cache/events accepts kind, key, text, session_id, run_id, scope, created_at_unix_ms
implementation.read_api: GET /v1/policy-cache/recent filters by kind, key, session_id, run_id and other caller scope fields
implementation.decision_api: POST /v1/policy-cache/decision returns deterministic recent-history trace for a supplied rule and scope
implementation.normal_chat_feed: POST /v1/chat/completions records a chat_request event scoped to session_id and run_id before policy evaluation

limitations: The cache is process-local and in-memory; restart loses history.
The prototype records chat history before policy evaluation, so the triggering chat request is included in its own recent window.
Regex matching uses Elixir Regex over stored event text and key only; it does not inspect arbitrary nested event value payloads.
No external persistence, multi-node coordination, or production admin authentication is implemented in this bakeoff slice.

manual: Changing the expected current run id in tests causes threshold assertions to stay below trigger.
Changing the literal match text causes history_count and alert_count assertions to fail.
Changing eviction order from created_at_unix_ms/sequence would retain oldest or wrong same-timestamp rows and fail the deterministic eviction test.
Changing recent_limit selection to insertion sequence only would count older matching events and fail the recent-window ordering test.
Moving chat event recording after policy evaluation would make the second chat request fail to trigger in the chat-feed test.

self_review.architecture: Agent serialization is adequate for this prototype and gives deterministic sequence allocation under concurrent writes. A production path would need durable storage plus authenticated admin endpoints.
self_review.code_quality: The history decision logic is concentrated in PolicyCache and Router only adapts caller scope and receipt trace. Existing cache_count remains for compatibility but new policies use history_decision.
self_review.test_quality: Tests assert externally visible behavior through Plug routes where practical and direct PolicyCache calls for regex/concurrency edge cases. They check failure-sensitive counts, scope, trace fields, and ordering rather than private implementation details.
self_review.security: No secrets, provider tokens, private endpoints, or raw real user content were added. Fixtures use synthetic session/run identifiers and synthetic prompt text.
